User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user in the CRM system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

authUid.description: The unique identifier from Firebase Authentication.

isActive.description: Indicates if the user account is active.

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the user account was created.

companyIds.description: References to Companies. (Relationship: User N:N Company)

Lead.title: Lead
Lead.description: Represents a lead in the CRM system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Lead entity.

productInterest.description: Product the lead is interested in.

status.description: Status of the lead (new, contacted, qualified, quoted, converted, lost).

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the lead was created.

lastContactAt.description: Timestamp of the last contact with the lead.

nextFollowUpAt.description: Timestamp of the next follow-up with the lead.

ExportOrder.title: ExportOrder
ExportOrder.description: Represents an export order in the CRM system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ExportOrder entity.

stage.description: Stage of the export order (leadReceived, quotationSent, orderConfirmed, exportDocumentation, shipmentReady, shippedDelivered, cancelled, lostNoResponse).

destinationCountry.description: Destination country for the export order.

hsCode.description: HS code for the export order.

quantity.description: Quantity of the product being exported.

unitPrice.description: Unit price of the product being exported.

paymentTerms.description: Payment terms for the export order.

containerType.description: Container type for the export order.

portOfLoading.description: Port of loading for the export order.

expectedShipmentDate.description: Expected shipment date for the export order.

fssaiLicenseNumber.description: FSSAI license number for the export order.

icegateStatus.description: Icegate status for the export order.

certificateRequirements.description: Certificate requirements for the export order.

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the export order was created.

aiValidation.description: AI validation suggestions for the export order.

Company.title: Company
Company.description: Represents a company in the CRM system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Company entity.

Contact.title: Contact
Contact.description: Represents a contact in the CRM system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Contact entity.

Task.title: Task
Task.description: Represents a task in the CRM system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Task entity.

status.description: Status of the task (open, inProgress, done).

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the task was created.

Document.title: Document
Document.description: Represents a document in the CRM system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Document entity.

type.description: Type of document (invoice, packingList, billOfLading, COO, certificate, other).

status.description: Status of the document (pending, uploaded, verified).

uploadedBy.description: Reference to User. (Relationship: User 1:N Document) - The user who uploaded the document.

uploadedAt.description: Timestamp of when the document was uploaded.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Uses path-based ownership for security. The `userId` MUST MATCH `authUid`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the user.

definition.description: Stores contacts associated with a company.

params.name: companyId
params.description: The ID of the company the contact belongs to.

definition.description: Stores leads associated with a company. Includes denormalized `companyId` for authorization independence.

params.name: companyId
params.description: The ID of the company the lead is associated with.

definition.description: Stores export orders associated with a company. Includes denormalized `companyId` for authorization independence.

params.name: companyId
params.description: The ID of the company the export order is associated with.

params.name: exportOrderId
params.description: Unique identifier for the export order.

definition.description: Stores tasks.  Does NOT include denormalized companyId. Admin access is required to list all tasks.
Stores documents. Does NOT include denormalized companyId. Admin access is required to list all documents.
Stores admin roles. Existence of a document grants admin privileges.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the admin user.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to prioritize authorization independence, clarity, and security. It leverages denormalization and structural segregation to ensure efficient and secure data access.

**Authorization Independence:** Each document contains all the necessary authorization information, eliminating the need for `get()` calls in security rules.  This is achieved primarily through denormalizing company IDs on user and user assignments on other documents. For example, Leads include the `companyId`, enabling rules to directly verify company membership.

**Structural Segregation:**  Data with different access requirements are stored in separate collections. For instance, user profiles are stored in `/users/{userId}`, enforcing path-based ownership.

**Access Modeling:**
*   **Path-Based Ownership:** User profiles (`/users/{userId}`) are secured using path-based ownership.
*   **Membership Map (not applicable here):** While not explicitly used, the structure anticipates future collaborative features.  If shared documents were added, a `members` map would be included within those documents.
*   **Global Roles:**  Admin roles are managed by the presence of documents in the `/roles_admin/{userId}` collection.  

**QAPs (Rules Are Not Filters):** The structure enables secure `list` operations. Sales Executives can list only leads assigned to them, and admins can list all leads. This is enforced through security rules without filtering on the client-side.

**Invariants:** Ownership and timestamps are enforced in the rules.

**Denormalization Strategy**
To eliminate `get()` calls and achieve authorization independence, the following denormalization strategies are applied:

*   **Company Access:** `companyId` is added to entities like leads and export orders that relate to companies. This allows security rules to directly check if a user has access to the company's data without needing to retrieve the company document. The `User` document includes an array of `companyIds` to define the companies a user belongs to.
*   **User Assignments:** Entities like `Lead`, and `ExportOrder` include an `assignedUserId` field which allows security rules to enforce that sales executives can only see their own assignments.
